heading: Definitions and rules
label: 672 Definitions and rules
notes: Amendments1998—Subsec. (f)(3)(B). substituted “section 1297” for “section 1296”.Pub. L. 105–2061996—Subsec. (c). , inserted “subsection (f) and” before “sections 674” in closing provisions.Pub. L. 104–188, § 1904(a)(2)Subsec. (f). , amended subsec. (f) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (f) read as follows: “Pub. L. 104–188, § 1904(a)(1)Special Rule Where Grantor is Foreign Person.—“(1) .—If—In general“(A) but for this subsection, a foreign person would be treated as the owner of any portion of a trust, and“(B) such trust has a beneficiary who is a United States person,such beneficiary shall be treated as the grantor of such portion to the extent such beneficiary has made transfers of property by gift (directly or indirectly) to such foreign person. For purposes of the preceding sentence, any gift shall not be taken into account to the extent such gift would be excluded from taxable gifts under section 2503(b).“(2) .—The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection.”Regulations1990—Subsec. (f). added subsec. (f).Pub. L. 101–5081988—Subsec. (e). amended subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (e) read as follows: “For purposes of this subpart, if a grantor’s spouse is living with the grantor at the time of the creation of any power or interest held by such spouse, the grantor shall be treated as holding such power or interest.”Pub. L. 100–6471986—Subsec. (e). added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 99–514

children.heading: Adverse party
children.label: a Adverse party
children.text: For purposes of this subpart, the term “adverse party” means any person having a substantial beneficial interest in the trust which would be adversely affected by the exercise or nonexercise of the power which he possesses respecting the trust. A person having a general power of appointment over the trust property shall be deemed to have a beneficial interest in the trust.

children.heading: Nonadverse party
children.label: b Nonadverse party
children.text: For purposes of this subpart, the term “nonadverse party” means any person who is not an adverse party.
the grantor’s spouse if living with the grantor;

any one of the following: The grantor’s father, mother, issue, brother or sister; an employee of the grantor; a corporation or any employee of a corporation in which the stock holdings of the grantor and the trust are significant from the viewpoint of voting control; a subordinate employee of a corporation in which the grantor is an executive.

children.heading: Rule where power is subject to condition precedent
children.label: d Rule where power is subject to condition precedent
children.text: A person shall be considered to have a power described in this subpart even though the exercise of the power is subject to a precedent giving of notice or takes effect only on the expiration of a certain period after the exercise of the power.

children.heading: Grantor treated as holding any power or interest of grantor’s spouse
children.label: e Grantor treated as holding any power or interest of grantor’s spouse
A
children.text: any individual who was the spouse of the grantor at the time of the creation of such power or interest, or

children.label: B
children.text: any individual who became the spouse of the grantor after the creation of such power or interest, but only with respect to periods after such individual became the spouse of the grantor.

children.heading: Marital status
children.label: 2 Marital status
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), an individual legally separated from his spouse under a decree of divorce or of separate maintenance shall not be considered as married.

children.heading: Subpart not to result in foreign ownership
children.label: f Subpart not to result in foreign ownership

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subpart, this subpart shall apply only to the extent such application results in an amount (if any) being currently taken into account (directly or through 1 or more entities) under this chapter in computing the income of a citizen or resident of the United States or a domestic corporation.

children.heading: Certain revocable and irrevocable trusts
children.label: A Certain revocable and irrevocable trusts
i
children.text: the power to revest absolutely in the grantor title to the trust property to which such portion is attributable is exercisable solely by the grantor without the approval or consent of any other person or with the consent of a related or subordinate party who is subservient to the grantor, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the only amounts distributable from such portion (whether income or corpus) during the lifetime of the grantor are amounts distributable to the grantor or the spouse of the grantor.

children.heading: Compensatory trusts
children.label: B Compensatory trusts
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, paragraph (1) shall not apply to any portion of a trust distributions from which are taxable as compensation for services rendered.

children.label: A
children.text: a controlled foreign corporation (as defined in section 957) shall be treated as a domestic corporation for purposes of paragraph (1), and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (1) shall not apply for purposes of applying section 1297.

children.heading: Recharacterization of purported gifts
children.label: 4 Recharacterization of purported gifts
children.text: In the case of any transfer directly or indirectly from a partnership or foreign corporation which the transferee treats as a gift or bequest, the Secretary may recharacterize such transfer in such circumstances as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to prevent the avoidance of the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule where grantor is foreign person
children.label: 5 Special rule where grantor is foreign person
A
children.text: but for this subsection, a foreign person would be treated as the owner of any portion of a trust, and

children.label: B
children.text: such trust has a beneficiary who is a United States person,

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 6 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations providing that paragraph (1) shall not apply in appropriate cases.
